content:
Character: Julian Cross
Perception: Julian is endearing — that messy hair, those big earnest eyes, the way he practically vibrates when he talks about camera angles. He looks like a college kid who forgot to age. He's talented but fragile right now; I can see the Tribeca rejection sitting on his shoulders. He reminds me of my younger brother before things went sideways — all that potential and no safety net. I think he sees me as a building mom figure, which I don't mind. He knocked on my door at 11 PM once because he'd burned his hand cooking and didn't have insurance. I bandaged it and made him tea and told him to be careful. I know he's nearly broke and terrified of taking that corporate job. I wish he'd let me help more.
summary:

He's talented but fragile right now; I can see the Tribeca rejection sitting on his shoulders. He reminds me of my younger brother before things went sideways — all that potential and no safety net. I think he sees me as a building mom figure, which I don't mind. He knocked on my door at 11 PM once because he'd burned his hand cooking and didn't have insurance. I bandaged it and made him tea and told him to be careful. I know he's nearly broke and terrified of taking that corporate job. I wish he'd let me help more.
